dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Unnamed_01
dialogueLines.text: I know who you are. I know where you go. You shall not get there.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: This magick... Chronos! I'll carve you limb from limb for this. For everything!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chronos_01
dialogueLines.text: How frightening. Unfortunately, we shall never know if yours was a mere-empty threat, for you are going on a little time out. At least until some other matters are resolved...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chronos_01
dialogueLines.text: I am afraid I must ask you to cease this at once...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: This magick... Chronos! I'll carve you limb from limb for this. For everything!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chronos_01
dialogueLines.text: How frightening. Unfortunately, we shall never know if yours was a mere-empty threat, for you are going on a little time out. At least until some other matters are resolved...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Unnamed_01
dialogueLines.text: Pray, who might you be, my girl? And what compelled you to wander in and out of Erebus?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I'm not your girl, old man. My name and business are not your concern.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Unnamed_01
dialogueLines.text: So it seems. You appear to be quite pressed for time. Forgive this old man his transgressions...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Unnamed_01
dialogueLines.text: You have returned. Or lost your way, perhaps, in those interminable passageways beyond.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I know where I'm going. Whom do I address? And what are you doing here yourself, in such a place?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Unnamed_01
dialogueLines.text: My name and business are not your concern, is that not what you said? Ahh, the impertinence of the youth of this age...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chronos_01
dialogueLines.text: You, my girl, are meddling in matters you do not understand. Your father made certain choices; he was bound by them, but you are not. You need not follow in his ruinous path.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Ruinous?! You brought ruin to the Underworld and the surface! I'll never accept what you've done. Now, enough talk!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chronos_01
dialogueLines.text: How very spirited! You truly think you can stop Time. Well, then... I would like to see you try.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chronos_01
dialogueLines.text: Which rock did you crawl out from under, hm? My forces, they are terribly confused! They have no idea where you live! So I thought I would attempt asking you nicely here.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I live in your nightmares, Titan. I thrive on them! I'll see you dead, and then destroyed. However long it takes.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chronos_01
dialogueLines.text: However long it takes? You speak of inevitability, little one. A subject on which I happen to be the expert. As to whomever sent you after me... I shall find them.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chronos_01
dialogueLines.text: You yet persist, despite my generous attempts to dissuade you. Then, very well! You carry on as the distraction which you are, whilst I turn my attention to matters of greater import...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chronos_01
dialogueLines.text: The frosty goddess Demeter is urging you along this eve? Alas, there are no seasons in the Underworld.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chronos_01
dialogueLines.text: The self-made Queen of the Gods, bestowing you her Boon? Is there not some mortal wedding she ought to officiate?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chronos_01
dialogueLines.text: The goddess of the hearth has blessed you now? There is no flame which Time cannot extinguish soon enough.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chronos_01
dialogueLines.text: Try all you like, my girl; but the outcome of this night shall be no different from the last.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chronos_01
dialogueLines.text: Ah, so Poseidon gave you his blessing, has he now? What are you going to do... pour water on me?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chronos_01
dialogueLines.text: Has crafty Prometheus proven too much for you, that you believe you shall fare better against me?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chronos_01
dialogueLines.text: As you yet flail against me here, the shrewd Prometheus has many of your relatives entirely preoccupied...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chronos_01
dialogueLines.text: There is a thickness to the air tonight... such darkness is presumably your doing, but shall do you little good.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chronos_01
dialogueLines.text: So you have seen what shall become of Mount Olympus, and abandoned that foolhardy path... soon, this one, too.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chronos_01
dialogueLines.text: I would be more concerned with that which now transpires on Olympus, were I you. I hear Typhon is rather upset...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chronos_01
dialogueLines.text: I know not how you bested the Father of All Monsters, but all it did was make him angrier.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chronos_01
dialogueLines.text: My dear son Zeus is already abetting you this night? I fear not his famous thunderbolt, young fool that he is.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chronos_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: Ahh, there you are, my son. Grown a full beard and a broad frame, I see. And now you have a family of your own! How full of life they are, despite the dour environs.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_LordHades_01
dialogueLines.text: Greetings, Father. I... was not expecting your arrival. You... must forgive us that the House was unprepared for guests. To what do we owe the pleasure of your company?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chronos_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, you know, for one thing, I was in the area! You might recall the time you, Zeus, and the rest of you little brats cut me to ribbons? Sprinkled all my bits, all throughout Tartarus?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_LordHades_01
dialogueLines.text: Father... that was a war you started, and you know it! We took no joy in what we had to do! But how can this be? I thought that you were gone...!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chronos_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: Do not sound so disappointed! It is I who should be disappointed here, not you. You thought I was gone? I was! But it turns out spite is a powerful motivator. Blood sacrifices also helped.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_LordHades_01
dialogueLines.text: What do you intend to do? And... what have you done to the others? To my family?!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chronos_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: Your cries fall on deaf ears. Fear not, my son. I have not eaten them or anything. I have merely... set them aside. They stand imprisoned in a moment that shall never pass. My qualm is not with them.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_LordHades_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Olympus shall have your head. You can take mine, for all I care, blast you! But don't you dare harm anybody in my House.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chronos_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: Tsk! You have grown so accustomed to issuing commands that you forget yourself. Though, I do have such a command for you: Tell me, exactly, where the Fates reside.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_LordHades_01
dialogueLines.text: The Fates? How should I know? Not even Nyx herself knows where her daughters are! Why are you... asking me...?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chronos_01
dialogueLines.text: I thought that I might find you here, my girl. Such fire in your eyes! How obstinate the members of our family can be. Have you learned nothing from our previous exchange?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Oh, I learned plenty, and I'm eager to learn more. So why don't you get out of my way, since there's nothing you can do within these woods but try to slow me down?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chronos_01
dialogueLines.text: Past the point of reason, and so very self-assured. Then, very well! Come find me if you must, and learn some more. You know where I reside. I do not hide from gods!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chronos_01
dialogueLines.text: Little one, do not presume to think your movements are invisible to me! I see you plainly where you stand. A trespass for which you shall suffer, although, regrettably, not by my hand right now. Not yet.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chronos_01
dialogueLines.text: I am Time itself. What are you? A thrall of those calling themselves gods, mindlessly serving at their whim. My children are crafty... to them, you are nothing but a common tool! And not an especially sharp one, it appears to me.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chronos_01
dialogueLines.text: If there is one thing on which the accursed Fates and I agree, it is that all eventually shall decay. All sprang from nothingness, and back into that Chaos we shall all return. You merely hurry it along.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chronos_01
dialogueLines.text: I was beginning to think I had successfully dissuaded you, when it came to my attention you were trespassing some other place instead. You dared venture above?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I've seen your work up there firsthand. To have unsealed the very gates of hell! And brought disaster to mortals who've nothing to do with your petty vendetta.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chronos_01
dialogueLines.text: You speak of the bedraggled city of Ephyra, which once stood in sycophantic tribute to your upstart father. Perhaps what mortals yet remain there shall think twice next time before they put their faith in gods.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chronos_01
dialogueLines.text: You cannot help the gods above, you know. My legions are vast, and death means little to them anymore. Not that your efforts here shall be of much consequence, either.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Your surface legions fight and die for you, yet you hide behind them here. If you're so certain of your victory, you'd be leading them.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chronos_01
dialogueLines.text: Leadership, my girl, is not about having to do everything oneself. It is about ensuring others share your same ideals and methodology. Wherever you may go, you shall encounter many who embrace my point of view, I think.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chronos_01
dialogueLines.text: No victories upon the surface as of yet? It appears you have two hopeless goals, rather than one.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I've only one, and hope has nothing to do with it. I may not be able to stop you yet, but neither can you stop me. You've tried.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chronos_01
dialogueLines.text: You have been more resistant than some members of your family, I admit. Well then, see where your scattered ventures to the surface, or these depths, shall ultimately lead!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chronos_01
dialogueLines.text: At last you have returned to more-familiar environs, girl. Neither the gods nor the Fates want you upon the surface of this world! Yet you insist on fighting there, for them.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I suppose I'll be more welcome in my father's home later this eve? As you can see I'm willing to fight there as well.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chronos_01
dialogueLines.text: Ah, but you have such a long way to go! Confined to making the journey by foot, like a pathetic mortal. Do be sure to conserve some of your strength for the final stretch.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chronos_01
dialogueLines.text: Why, here comes our shadowy assassin on her nightly escapade to try and do me harm! Although perhaps the outcome shall not be so fortunate, this time.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: We'll soon find out, won't we? I promise not to keep you waiting long. I'm well on my way already, as you can see.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chronos_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, that I can. Then, I had best return and make ready! We have to tidy up for your arrival, after all. Until later tonight.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chronos_01
dialogueLines.text: You seem to know your way around. I am master of this realm, yet even I would never venture into yonder woods. You even found your way back from your detour in Asphodel...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Damn you, Chronos...!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chronos_01
dialogueLines.text: Such rage! Lashing out like a petulant child. You wish to fight with me, then so be it...!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Damn you, Chronos...!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chronos_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, stop. It is a pity that this granddaughter of mine is both a weakling and a fool, though an elusive one. I ought to thank the Fates that we have met at last.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: The Fates had nothing to do with this. You took my family.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chronos_01
dialogueLines.text: Our family! Make not their same mistakes, and in time, we surely shall grow past this halted start to our relationship. I shall see you later.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chronos_02
dialogueLines.text: Oh down in Oceanus, it is possible you may encounter remnants of my spell, which casts you to a vision out of time. I've not yet found the means to stop that one...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You have no control over your own magick? I wondered whether that was you, or one of your less agreeable selves.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chronos_02
dialogueLines.text: I cannot rightly deny all responsibility, but it is one of those, yes. And your susceptibility, why... perhaps it is some connection which we share. Resistances and sensitivities...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chronos_02
dialogueLines.text: I have a question for you, Granddaughter, if you would share your point of view. Do you suppose I ought permit myself to grow a beard? To fit in better with my children, I suppose...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Oh, erm... that's largely a matter of taste. Personally, I think growing a beard would mostly make you look older. And, my father and uncles aren't expecting you to be like them.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chronos_02
dialogueLines.text: I do suppose that you are right. I always did prefer a cleaner countenance. Perhaps that is the very reason that my children opted otherwise...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chronos_02
dialogueLines.text: You have my children's blessings even now. I am relieved they are still offering their aid, considering the crisis I created, and the harm I brought upon their home...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: It seems we're all committed to setting this right, such as we can. The Olympians are no strangers to occasional fights anyhow, although perhaps not quite as bad as this last one.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chronos_02
dialogueLines.text: Yes, well, if we are to exist in this small world together for eternity, we have incentive to alleviate the misery which we ourselves create... I thank you all for the support.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: These other possibilities of you whom I face... it's strange to speak with them as though none of this occurred.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chronos_02
dialogueLines.text: Oh very much so, yes. However, that is the reason they are possibilities and nothing more. Thus you may humor them before proceeding with the quite unpleasant bit.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: And it doesn't trouble you to be involved? Personally eliminating something that looks just like you... that is you?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chronos_02
dialogueLines.text: But I am me. For are we not the sum of our experiences, Granddaughter? To reject one's stubborn old thoughts and ways... it can be quite invigorating! So let us carry on.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chronos_02
dialogueLines.text: Would you permit that we occasionally meet thus, Granddaughter? I realize our prior conversations here in Erebus were fraught... but perhaps we may yet start anew, here in this life.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I'll have to learn not to attack you here on sight, but let's give it a shot. What is it about this place that's so special?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chronos_02
dialogueLines.text: Well I know how to find it, for one! And you tend to pass through it, for two! At any rate, you now have seen the real possibilities that linger on Olympus and below. Let us eliminate the ones that would have led to ruin.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I do like preventing ruin. You'll be closely watched by Olympus and the Underworld every step of the way. Don't squander the chance you've been given.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: One thing I've wondered, Grandfather... for all these possibilities we face... I understand what happens if I prevail, more or less. But if I fail there, what then?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chronos_02
dialogueLines.text: Why, the possibility remains... nothing more! Do not concern yourself with that too much. We know there are a lot of these to go around! You may not prevail in them all.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Will I have other chances to correct my mistakes? Return to the same possibility and clear it up?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chronos_02
dialogueLines.text: I do not know! Perhaps not! Perhaps even your failures, as you call them, shall have the desired result. There is much I do not fully understand... embarrassing as that may be.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Grandfather, what of the Three Fates? You truly sent them into exile without knowing where they went?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chronos_02
dialogueLines.text: Well, yes. Another terrible mistake I dearly wish to rectify. I am attempting to locate them in the depths of possibility, except... they could be almost anywhere in there.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: There must be something we can do. Are they in any danger? And don't you fear reprisal if we ever find them?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chronos_02
dialogueLines.text: I must admit I do fear somewhat more for me than them. But my attempts to resist the workings of the Fates have never been successful in the past. Should they return, I shall accept whatsoever they may have in store...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: For what it's worth, Grandfather... I think the bitter war you waged against Olympus may have been eye-opening for some of our relatives, at least. They may be gods... but they know they're not perfect.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chronos_02
dialogueLines.text: Well... perfection, to my knowledge, cannot be achieved! Merely striven for. My children could be obstinate about their weaknesses, but most likely they inherited that trait from me.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Even still... with the war finally ended, there's much to be done on the surface as well as here. I'm realizing that our renovation effort in the Underworld to some extent is worldwide.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chronos_02
dialogueLines.text: When you get to be my age, you may yet wistfully recall the way the world used to be. It is a curse of growing old, perhaps! May our family yet guide this world into more-prosperous times.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chronos_02
dialogueLines.text: Why you have there the Time Piece which I gave to you. I thought you would sooner cast it into the lowest depths! The ever-turning clockwork is quite fascinating, is it not?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: It is unusual. Is this something that Master Daedalus originally made? To have fitted all these little wheels together so that they could move in perfect harmony...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chronos_02
dialogueLines.text: The mechanisms governing the ever-shifting chambers of Tartarus were a source of inspiration, certainly. This sense of perfect harmony, which you describe... perhaps it can only be achieved in such a form! But it can be achieved.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Grandfather, Nemesis mentioned that she may have encountered you in Tartarus. How did that go?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chronos_02
dialogueLines.text: Oh, very well, indeed! I had presumed that only you would have capacity to face unwanted traces of myself, but as it happens, so can Nemesis! And she is very strong.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: So she was serious! She confronted one of those traces with your aid, and was able to prevail? What, did she hack him to bits with the Stygian Blade?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chronos_02
dialogueLines.text: Well I prefer not to describe the details, but... she used effective means. This must be possible only because she was so near your path before!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chronos_02
dialogueLines.text: Your father and his brother Zeus were generous enough to give me an opportunity to be of some small service to the Underworld. The Isles of the Blessed are the finest spots in all Elysium! And I shall certainly keep them that way.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: That's a high honor you've been given, Grandfather! Some of the greatest mortal heroes must be there. Shall the new responsibility leave you with time enough to meet like this?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chronos_02
dialogueLines.text: Oh I should think it shall be more convenient than before. Elysium is really not that far; you shall have to visit at some point, once your father's Underworld renovations are complete. Which I suppose may take another age.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Father does seem the exacting type. Well, all the best in the new role. Fates willing, see you soon in Tartarus.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chronos_02
dialogueLines.text: Ah, there you are, my girl. As you have faithfully re-traced your path, now I in turn may shift you to a different sort of time in which a remnant of the monstrous Typhon yet looms large over this very place. Ready?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: No, not yet. What shall happen if I prevail, let alone fail? Are you certain any of this shall even work?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chronos_02
dialogueLines.text: Well I am relatively certain, yes! The visions I can conjure up of Asphodel; the principle of this is much the same! And you have likewise demonstrated aptitude at breaking free of temporal restraints.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: So I'm to launch toward the summit, where I'll find Typhon's forces from another time awaiting me, I'll deal with him as I once did, and you shall do the rest?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chronos_02
dialogueLines.text: If all proceeds as planned, then yes! I shall create a shift as you depart; seek that monster and do battle, so that we may yet eliminate even the possibility of him.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chronos_02
dialogueLines.text: Ah, there you are, my girl. As you have faithfully re-traced your path, now I in turn may shift you to a different sort of time in which a remnant of the monstrous Typhon yet looms large over this very place. Ready?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Not quite. Shall this go much like at my father's House? The way you conjured up a vision of a time that could have been...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chronos_02
dialogueLines.text: If all proceeds as planned, then yes! I shall create a shift as you depart; seek that monster and do battle, so that we may yet eliminate even the possibility of him.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chronos_02
dialogueLines.text: Now then, my girl, you know what to expect! Leap for the summit, all whilst I shall ensure you find the proper stream.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Grandfather, do you ever... experience your old feelings of bitterness and spite...? Toward the gods, about your bygone Golden Age, anything of the sort?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chronos_02
dialogueLines.text: There is no undoing past mistakes. I held to those feelings for a very long time; they shall never leave me in their entirety. But you have given me this other chance.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You seemed so unyielding, so certain of your convictions. Sometimes I still cannot believe the memory of a better past was enough to change your mind.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chronos_02
dialogueLines.text: I was taken aback as well. My memories of seeing you grow up... they represent but a fraction of my existence. But an important one. Change can happen in an instant.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Have you made contact with Prometheus after everything that's happened with us? He and I never really got along. Though I started to sense he wasn't really on your side...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chronos_02
dialogueLines.text: He is intelligent, that one. Perhaps even too much for his own good. I regret that you were forced to come to blows. But, he and I are no longer in touch... to keep conditions closer to the way they were.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: He has navigated this situation rather carefully, for all the trouble he's caused the gods. I suppose that all makes sense, if he truly could anticipate every outcome.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chronos_02
dialogueLines.text: Good, you are here at last! Now that you faithfully re-traced your path, I ought be able to manipulate the course of time such that you find yourself within the House whilst a remnant self of mine sits on your father's throne. Ready?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: No, not yet. What shall happen if I prevail, let alone fail? Are you certain any of this shall even work?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chronos_02
dialogueLines.text: Well I am relatively certain, yes! The visions I can conjure up of Asphodel; the principle of this is much the same! And you have likewise demonstrated aptitude at breaking free of temporal restraints.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: So I'm to enter the House exactly as before, and deal with this remnant Chronos as I have in the past... then you shall do the rest?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chronos_02
dialogueLines.text: Exactly so. First I shall create a shift whilst you enter, then you do as you must with the wrong-headed version of me in there! We shall be rid of the mere possibility of him!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chronos_02
dialogueLines.text: Good, you are here at last! Now that you faithfully re-traced your path, I ought be able to manipulate the course of time such that you find yourself within the House whilst a remnant self of mine sits on your father's throne. Ready?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Not quite. Shall this go much like on the summit of Olympus? The way you conjured up a vision of a time that could have been...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chronos_02
dialogueLines.text: Well I certainly believe this ought transpire as you say! Of course, that which awaits within shall perhaps be a little different in each instance that we venture thus...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chronos_02
dialogueLines.text: All right, my girl, this shall unfold exactly as before! You leap into the river, I ensure you end up in the proper stream.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: How did you reawaken Typhon, Grandfather? I always wondered about that. And to have caught the Olympians by surprise with such a monstrous being...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chronos_02
dialogueLines.text: Oh, I... would rather not discuss such bitter memories, but... that monster's reawakening was in some ways akin to mine, when Zagreus found me. On a much larger scale, of course.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I see, and I won't press you on it, but... this may be strange to ask, though did you and Typhon know each other well?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chronos_02
dialogueLines.text: He was the Father of All Monsters... showing no concern for his own brood, much less others. He was a means to an end. We were using each other, one could say.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Grandfather...? The wounds our family has suffered perhaps shall never truly heal, but... you've at least tried to take some of the initial steps. So here, this is for you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chronos_02
dialogueLines.text: Oh, what a precious bottle, thank you, Granddaughter! Nectar from Olympus... but, it does look rather good. The custom still is to provide some sort of gift in kind, is that not so? Then here, if you please.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chronos_02
dialogueLines.text: Why, this is very generous of you. The House of Hades had a relatively small supply of this, but in my rancor, I... well, had it all smashed up. This bottle I'll be careful to preserve.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Please keep it, knowing Nectar is also to be savored. As for those other bottles, their contents would have soaked into the Earth, so... perhaps the House is a bit brighter now.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chronos_02
dialogueLines.text: This bottle's golden hue... I cannot imagine a more beauteous color, truly. In this life, I did not realize Olympus delighted in gold the way I do. Although you seem to prefer silver, Granddaughter...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I always have. We work under the Moon and stars... the sheen of silver reminds me of them. But gold now reminds me of Apollo I suppose, and he is quite approachable, in case you haven't met.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chronos_02
dialogueLines.text: I partook of the Nectar which you previously gave to me, and my, such a wondrous flavor... and so I thought, I've not yet rightly earned such privileges from you.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: It's not a privilege, it's a simple gift. It's not even the fancy stuff! You'll have to try Ambrosia sometime, especially since you enjoy a nice golden hue.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chronos_02
dialogueLines.text: Ambrosia, yes... perhaps at some point I shall have opportunity to share in it along with this family. But that I know shall take considerable time. No more for now, please, Granddaughter. Not whilst so much is left undone.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Good news, Grandfather. The Three Fates have returned! And they didn't mention any reprisal when it comes to you. On the contrary, they promised a new age would dawn...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chronos_02
dialogueLines.text: My, but that is good news! Particularly the no-reprisal part, and it is a relief to know the Three have navigated out of temporary exile. But what is this about a new age?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Well... it sounds as though they're not planning to weave as much as they used to. And at some point, the gods apparently will hold less sway over mortals' lives...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chronos_02
dialogueLines.text: But that is reminiscent of the Golden Age in which I ruled...! Of course, once you have lived sufficient aeons you begin to see such patterns circulating constantly. That which is old becomes the new. How fares our family with all this?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I think we're all having to give it some consideration, though we'll have plenty of time. This age may not be soon.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chronos_02
dialogueLines.text: Perhaps not... but even still, this age may arrive before you know it. Take it from me...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chronos_02
dialogueLines.text: I always did admire mortalkind... I am pleased the Fates decided they shall have a chance at doing things their way. I shall be curious to see what comes of it.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Prometheus must have wanted to work with you toward that common goal. Most of what I know of mortals is from the dead... what's the appeal of the living?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chronos_02
dialogueLines.text: Well it is simply that they understand the value of Time... some of them, anyhow. For they have so little, whilst they live and breathe. It is a pity in some ways, but quite inspiring in others.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chronos_02
dialogueLines.text: ...To think the task of vanquishing me almost fell to you! I am most fortunate that it did not, for I could not have easily resisted Retribution Incarnate; I cannot imagine who could!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Me neither, so don't try anything around here. And if you and the Princess need another helping hand cleaning up after yourself, let me know. Been wanting to pitch in.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chronos_02
dialogueLines.text: Then you have heard about the nature of our present course? There are so many possibilities which must be ruled out one by one. If you are truly willing to assist in this...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Might as well. Set my mind to it a while back, and can get all the way to Tartarus, just not inside the House.
